channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Bubba AI, Inc. is launching Comp AI to help 100,000 startups get SOC 2 compliant by 2032
item.description: With the growing importance of security compliance for startups, more companies are seeking to achieve and maintain compliance with frameworks like SOC 2, ISO 27001 & GDPR. Bubba AI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: EV-maker BYD seeks to raise $5.2 billion with share sale
item.description: Chinese electric vehicle maker BYD has launched a sale of its Hong Kong shares to raise up to $5.2 billion via an accelerated book-building, according to a deal term sheet seen by Reuters on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: This waterproof tablet can do something no Apple iPad can, make calls on any one of two SIM cards - and it is cheap as chips
item.description: The BMAX MaxPad I10 S Pro is a rugged tablet with dual SIM support, full mobile connectivity, and waterproofing - all for less than $200.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google is working on its own version of Apple’s Hide My Email, and you might soon be able to try it yourself
item.description: Google is reportedly working on a new Shielded Email feature that will hide your email address when signing up for accounts, similar to the existing Hide My Email feature offered by Apple.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Americans split over Musk, federal workforce cuts: Survey
item.description: Americans in a new survey are split over their views of Elon Musk and his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), as well as federal workforce cuts during the opening weeks of the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s data protection watchdog said Monday that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Galaxy Buds 3 Pro just got 5 much-needed upgrades, but you need a Galaxy S25 to get ‘em
item.description: Your Galaxy Buds 3 Pro and Buds 3 are about to get 5 much-needed upgrades, but only if you’re running One UI 7 (which currently, is limited to the Galaxy S25).
item.dc:creator: becky.scarrott@futurenet.com (Becky Scarrott), TechRadar

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra could have even smaller bezels - and that could mean an even bigger display
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra has been tipped to come with even smaller bezels than the Galaxy S25 Ultra, which is already near enough all screen – that extra shrinkage could mean a 7-inch Galaxy display is on the way.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Bad onboarding is costing businesses billions - here's how you can improve
item.description: A bad employee onboarding experience could be costing US businesses more than they realize thanks to broader knock-on effects.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Leaked Galaxy Tab S10 FE specs and unofficial renders hint at how Samsung will take on the iPad again
item.description: We've got some more details about the upcoming Samsung Galaxy Tab S10 FE, including specs and pictures.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Your iPhone has a hidden folder eating up storage space without you even knowing
item.description: iPhones have a hidden folder that eats up storage without you knowing it. Kurt the CyberGuy offers some tricks to free up storage on your device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Private lunar lander Blue Ghost aces moon touchdown with a special delivery for NASA
item.description: A private lunar lander has touched down on the moon, delivering a drill and other experiments for NASA.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DOGE structure, authority emerge as biggest DC mystery under Trump
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) structure and authority has become a central mystery in the barrage of lawsuits seeking to stymie its operations.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: Investment research data breach exposes 12 million customers
item.description: A recent ransomeware attack targeted Zacks, an American investment firm, and a cybercriminal claimed to have stolen records for millions of customers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intel’s anticipated $28 billion chip factories in Ohio are delayed until 2030
item.description: Intel‘s promised $28 billion chip fabrication plants in Ohio are facing further delays, with the first factory in New Albany expected to not be completed until 2030, local media outlet The Columbus Dispatch reported on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google co-founder: Be in the office every weekday, work 60 hours a week
item.description: Google co-founder Sergey Brin sent an internal message Wednesday to the group working on the company’s AI model Gemini. In the message, Brin wrote that Google can become a leader in AI development — provided that employees work more.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google’s AI summaries are changing search. Now it’s facing a lawsuit
item.description: For more than two decades, users have turned to search engines like Google, typed in a query, and received a familiar list of 10 blue links—the gateway to the wider web. Ranking high on that list, through search engine optimization (SEO), has become a $200 billion business.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: East Bay startup’s new stove that plugs into a normal wall outlet could be major gain for health and climate
item.description: The sleek, standard four-burner electric induction stove runs on 120 volts, meaning there is no need to pay a licensed electrician thousands of dollars to rewire to 240 volts, which many electric stoves require.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Gabbard decries Britain's reported demand for Apple to provide backdoor access to users' cloud data
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard says she has serious concerns about the British government's reported demand that Apple provide backdoor access to any data stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Critical infrastructure: The rising ransomware victim
item.description: Ferhat Dikbiyik, the chief research and intelligence officer at Black Kite, explains how critical infrastructure providers can better protect their systems. The post Critical infrastructure: The rising ransomware victim first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Signal will exit Sweden rather than dilute message security
item.description: The CEO of Signal said Tuesday that the service will leave Sweden rather than comply with a rule that will require vendors to capture all secure messages and save a plain text copy, in case authorities later want to subpoena that data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Get started with async in Python
item.description: Asynchronous programming, or async, is a feature of many modern languages that allows a program to juggle multiple operations without waiting or getting hung up on any one of them.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Plug-and-play web development with Astro
item.description: Astro is a full-stack JavaScript meta-framework that orchestrates reactive view technologies like React and Svelte. While there are numerous meta-frameworks (examples include Next.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI, automation spur efforts to upskill network pros
item.description: Networking skills are evolving to keep pace with broader technology trends, and network pros need to proactively develop new competencies in areas such as AI and automation to stay relevant.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Precise asset management leads to impactful zero trust initiatives
item.description: Agencies’ attention and efforts are now shifting towards strengthening cybersecurity defenses to address the more challenging aspects of cyber threats. The post Precise asset management leads to impactful zero trust initiatives first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network
